lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Fuck youuuu, fuck youuuu. Fuck you up there! Fuck you! "Fuck you." Those words mean a great deal to us. They help us express just how we as a company... see things differently. There are a lot of startup companies on Kickstarter, but after today I think you'll agree that Washington Redskins is the most exciting. As you know, the Redskins have been on the forefront of Kickstarter as a company that is always finding new and exciting ways to tell people to go fuck themselves. And now, our company is thrilled to show you all the latest innovations we've come up with.  To begin with, we have moved the couch from the left side of the office to the right side. But we didn't stop there. We also added a new rug that goes better with our office drapes. And probably most exciting of all, we have actually updated the company from the inside out. We received a lot of pressure from certain communities to change our Redskins logo. As a company we want to be firm, but we also want to be flexible. And so we thought, if we have to change our logo, it should be more in step with today's times, but still define us as the leading Kickstarter company. What we came up with is the new company logo that I think you'll all agree is very exciting.  Now when people hear the name "Redskins", they will immediately think "titties" and "balls". It's just a bold new way that we can say "We don't fucking care." Go Redskins!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: It's so awesome, dude. Our company already has a hundred backers. And we still have 14 days to go!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I've known Clyde Frog since I was two years old. He was a fighter, a visionary. But most of all, Clyde Frog was the perfect friend. He never said anything. He never had his own ideas about what he wanted to do. He just sat there goin' along with whatever I wanted. A noble trait that seems lost on you 99%ers.  And now we live in a world where one of you has decided to become a murderer. Well whoever you are, I'm sure you're sitting there right now, in your little green hat, with your left cheek resting against your hand thinking, "I'm not getting away with this, am I?" Because whoever you are, maybe you still have one little piece of humanity left, covered up and tainted black by years of Jewish propaganda and left-wing lies. Maybe you should just-

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Shut the hell up, Cartman!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Got something to say Kyle?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: How do you murder a stuffed animal?!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: How do you murder a stuffed animal?! You tell us, Kyle!  Butbut but but, but uh but let's not get ahead of ourselves. There's still a biiig investigation to come. And until we prove beyond a reasonable doubt who the killer was, you are all just as guilty as Kyle. Thank you so much. Bagpipes please.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: You have got to be kidding me!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: What, Kyle?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: We should not be wasting time and money making sure Cartman's fat scooter can't be tipped over!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You condone Rascal-tipping, Kyle?! Do you know that all over our country people in mobility scooters are being victimized?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: So get off your fucking scooters and pick them up!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Oh! Oh, did you hear him?!  This is exactly what Adele is talking about!  Our culture celebrates anorexia and tries to make us ashamed if we don't have slim stomachs and perky little tits like Kyle!  Well I for one am gonna stop feeling ashamed just because Kyle throws up in the bathroom six times a day  to make sure he looks good on his magazine covers!  You should be ashamed of YOURself, Kyle! eating your latkes and gefilte fish and then putting your fingers down your throat because society tells you how to look!  I'm not feeling shamed anymore! From now on, I'm gonna shop sitting down and skip to the front of the lines with my head held high! Just because someone needs a mobility scooter doesn't mean they're an ignorant piece of white trash, Kyle! I may be fat, but I'm not Honey Boo Boo!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: What, Kyle?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: We should not be wasting time and money making sure Cartman's fat scooter can't be tipped over!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You condone Rascal-tipping, Kyle?! Do you know that all over our country people in mobility scooters are being victimized?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: So get off your fucking scooters and pick them up!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Oh! Oh, did you hear him?!  This is exactly what Adele is talking about!  Our culture celebrates anorexia and tries to make us ashamed if we don't have slim stomachs and perky little tits like Kyle!  Well I for one am gonna stop feeling ashamed just because Kyle throws up in the bathroom six times a day  to make sure he looks good on his magazine covers!  You should be ashamed of YOURself, Kyle! eating your latkes and gefilte fish and then putting your fingers down your throat because society tells you how to look!  I'm not feeling shamed anymore! From now on, I'm gonna shop sitting down and skip to the front of the lines with my head held high! Just because someone needs a mobility scooter doesn't mean they're an ignorant piece of white trash, Kyle! I may be fat, but I'm not Honey Boo Boo!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Who's Honey Boo Boo?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: It's simple television economics, Kyle. All it takes to kill a show forever is get one episode pulled. If we convince the network to pull this episode for the sake of Muslims, then the Catholics can demand a show they don't like get pulled. And then people with disabilities can demand another show get pulled. And so on and so on, until Family Guy is no more! It's exactly what happened to Laverne & Shirley.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Whoa whoa wait a minute!  This isn't what I signed up for! I like Family Guy. Why do we have to get it off the air forever?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Because they made f-fun of Muslims, and and that's wrong.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: But that doesn't mean it has to go off the air. You should like that show. Your sense of humor is just like Family Guy.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Don't you EVER, EVER, compare me to Family Guy! You hear me Kyle??!! Compare me to Family Guy again, and so help me I will kill you where you stand!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: You unbelievable son of a bitch. You never cared about the Muslim religion. Or the safety of people in America. You just want Family Guy off the air!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Guys, we need to talk to you. I really don't think we wanna be a company that ISIS looks up to. We should maybe issue a statement saying that we don't sanction them.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Oh no. No, you guys. We started this company to do nothing. If we start doing stuff now, it'll put it all at risk!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: People aren't gonna support our company if we dig in our heels and say we don't care about anything!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Digging in our heels and pissing on public opinion us what the Washington Redskins are all about!  Now come on, guys! If you wanna be a successful business, then you have to be honest about what you are!  Once you take a stand on something, you're pretending like your company is about more than money. Then all of a sudden you're the NFL and and your players get caught molesting little boys!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: That's the Catholic Church!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: NFL, Catholic Church, same thing! Okay, let's use the Catholic Church! You take a moral stand on issues, you say you're about honor and integrity, and the next thing you know, your clergymen are getting caught beating up their wives in an elevator!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Dude, fuck Facebook! Seriously!  What the hell is this?! Podcast?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Welcome to Cartman's Incredible Podcast!  Hello fellow Facebookers, I'm here to do one thing: get you more friends!  Looking around Facebook today we see that since adding loser Kip Drordy as a friend, Kyle Broflovski's stock is plummeting! He had 55 friends just two days ago, he's down to just 11 this morning. Run to your Facebook account and delete Kyle from your friend list because he is poison and I don't see him making a comeback any time soon.  You're gonna want to dump Kyle and if at all possible add Clyde Donovan. Why?  Birthday! That's right, Clyde has a birthday coming up and his mom is taking everyone to Casa Bonita.  If you don't have Clyde as a friend, you're gonna want to add him, because Clyde's numbers are about to go way up! And now, Word On The Street.  The rumors are now becoming more than that; Jimmy and Bebe have agreed to share their friends. That's right. Looks like we're about to have a merger  So if you're a friend of Jimmy's you're about to luck into about 90 chick friends, and as we all know, chick friends are worth almost triple what dude friends are. That's all the time I have for today. Remember, update that profile, and steer clear of Kyle.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Eh, so, you don't want to make your parents suffer and pay for mistreating you, then?  Look, ah, I don't know what your parents did to you, but if you're here, I take it they pissed you off pretty good. Maybe you should at least ...hear what I have to offer?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Oh, sorry. I thought this was a legitimate business!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: That it is, I assure you. I started this business over three months ago from the ground up. I've been helping children get back at their parents ever since.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: How many parents have you exacted revenge upon?!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Craig's. a-and Clyde's. Oh and and Kyle's, but that was a freebie. Look, I run a legitimate business here with state-of-the-art computers, charts, and technology. Look around you. I know how it feels to be really, really pissed off at your parents. And I will work hard, for you.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Our moms and dads lied to us about those future selves! It was just a trick to get us to not wanna try drugs or alcohol.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Dude, aren't you worried? Wendy's a girl. She could end up getting really hurt, Stan. You're my bro, and she's your bitch. I don't wanna hurt my bro's bitch.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: I can't do anything; she really wants to fight you.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Nu-no, she doesn't. She told me she doesn't wanna fight. She's just stuck, Stan. Because she knows I'm gonna kick her ass, but she feels like if she doesn't fight me all the kids are gonna call her a chicken.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You know what she did? She apologized and begged me not to fight her. I said "Well Wendy, if you're so sorry, then say it in front of everyone." But she wouldn't. That's how desperate she is. Man you have to do something, Stan. You have to be a fuckin' man and forbid her from fighting, so she has a way-  Oh, that's where I put those.  Anyway, Stan, you've got to put a stop to this fight. Wendy's stuck and she needs you.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Dude, there's nothing I can do about it.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: We think the movie sucked and we want Mel Gibson to give us back our eighteen dollars. Do you know how we can get in touch with him?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You thought it sucked? Sir, apparently, you don't understand what Mel Gibson was trying to do! He was trying to express, through cinema, the horror and filthiness of the common Jew. It has made people the world over open their eyes.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Look, kid, we just thought it was a bad movie, so tell us how to get in touch with Mel Gibson so we can get our money back!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: If I knew where Mel Gibson was, I'd be down on the floor licking his balls at this very moment, sir. All I know is he lives somewhere in Malibu. Now stop wasting me and Mel Gibson's time, you little wussy prick.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Hey, don't take that tone with me, kid! I'll kick your ass!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Oh yeah?! I'd like to see you try, asshole! I'm like six feet tall!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Dude, aren't you worried? Wendy's a girl. She could end up getting really hurt, Stan. You're my bro, and she's your bitch. I don't wanna hurt my bro's bitch.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: I can't do anything; she really wants to fight you.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Nu-no, she doesn't. She told me she doesn't wanna fight. She's just stuck, Stan. Because she knows I'm gonna kick her ass, but she feels like if she doesn't fight me all the kids are gonna call her a chicken.

lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You know what she did? She apologized and begged me not to fight her. I said "Well Wendy, if you're so sorry, then say it in front of everyone." But she wouldn't. That's how desperate she is. Man you have to do something, Stan. You have to be a fuckin' man and forbid her from fighting, so she has a way-  Oh, that's where I put those.  Anyway, Stan, you've got to put a stop to this fight. Wendy's stuck and she needs you.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Nu-no, she doesn't. She told me she doesn't wanna fight. She's just stuck, Stan. Because she knows I'm gonna kick her ass, but she feels like if she doesn't fight me all the kids are gonna call her a chicken.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You know what she did? She apologized and begged me not to fight her. I said "Well Wendy, if you're so sorry, then say it in front of everyone." But she wouldn't. That's how desperate she is. Man you have to do something, Stan. You have to be a fuckin' man and forbid her from fighting, so she has a way-  Oh, that's where I put those.  Anyway, Stan, you've got to put a stop to this fight. Wendy's stuck and she needs you.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Dude, there's nothing I can do about it.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: God, you are such a pussy, Stan! You're such a pussy! When I hurt Wendy, it's gonna be on your fucking head!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Kyle?  Dude, what are you doing here? Everybody's looking for you. Kyle?

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Do you know what happened to me this morning, Stan? This morning... I woke up and felt a sharp pain in my ass. I felt down there and, and found this... big sore lump. On my ass, Stan. I couldn't even sit down, so... I, I had to tell my mother, which, which was humiliating. She took me to the doctor, which was more humiliating, a- a- nd he told me. I-I have a hemorrhoid. It's like an infected blood vessel on your ass. I'm nine years old, and I have a hemorrhoid, Stan. I have a hemorrhoid, and, Cartman has his own theme park.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Kyle, I, I understand what you mean, but--

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Do you?! Do you, Stan?! Because all my life I was raised to believe in Jehovah! To believe that we should all behave a certain way and good things will come to us. I make mistakes, but every week I try to better myself. I'm always saying, "You know, I learned something today..." and what does this so-called God give me in return? A hemorrhoid. He doesn't make sense!  What is your logic?!  Ow.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Look. Cartman... he thinks he's gonna be happy because he has his own amusement park, but, he's gonna find out that without other people, the rides are totally lame. I mean, who could really have fun by themselves at a theme park? I'll bet he's sick of it already.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Do you know what happened to me this morning, Stan? This morning... I woke up and felt a sharp pain in my ass. I felt down there and, and found this... big sore lump. On my ass, Stan. I couldn't even sit down, so... I, I had to tell my mother, which, which was humiliating. She took me to the doctor, which was more humiliating, a- a- nd he told me. I-I have a hemorrhoid. It's like an infected blood vessel on your ass. I'm nine years old, and I have a hemorrhoid, Stan. I have a hemorrhoid, and, Cartman has his own theme park.

lines.speaker: Stan

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Do you?! Do you, Stan?! Because all my life I was raised to believe in Jehovah! To believe that we should all behave a certain way and good things will come to us. I make mistakes, but every week I try to better myself. I'm always saying, "You know, I learned something today..." and what does this so-called God give me in return? A hemorrhoid. He doesn't make sense!  What is your logic?!  Ow.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Look. Cartman... he thinks he's gonna be happy because he has his own amusement park, but, he's gonna find out that without other people, the rides are totally lame. I mean, who could really have fun by themselves at a theme park? I'll bet he's sick of it already.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Because you kind of made it matter.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: You know, I'm just, I'm just through with this whole thing! I just, I can't put my family through any more of this!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: S-so you did superglue it.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Ugh, you know what's really sad?! That this is what matters to people! What nobody seems to remember are the farmers in Belarus! STANground is bigger than me! You wanna tell all those farmers in Belarus it's all bullcrap?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Dude, the sooner you eat your peas, the sooner you can try to win some of your friends back. Eh ya you need to Bill Clinton this.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: You really think I went through all that trouble, lying about my wristband and claiming to be something I wasn't, for what, Kyle?! For what?!  This is a witch hunt, dude! It's all that stupid French scientist! He's the fraud, and I'm gonna prove it!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: I mean, I do, but, well, ever since I became friends with that Kip Drordy kid, a bunch of my other friends have started ignoring me. It would be fine except for my farm is starting to shrink. I know that I should just dump Kip as a friend, but that's such a terrible thing to do and, I'm sorry I'm just so confused I... I really need a friend right now.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Okay dude, I'm I'm here for you.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: 'K, so then get on Facebook and fertilize my crops?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: NO.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Pleahehese! My farm hasn't expanded in three days!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Dude, I've already had to become friends with all of Wendy's frends and my grandma's friends! I do NOT want to start doing all the farming stuff too! I'm not getting sucked into that!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Oh, thank you! I was so eagerly awaiting your approval of my abilities!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Look, Kyle, the game is still set up at my house and, maybe we could go try playing it again over there.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Oh, so the gallant knight now comes to rescue me from the bowels of mediocrity! Oh, thank you, your royal lordship!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: That isn't it at all.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: You don't get it, Stan! I can play here all I want. I even get free Frescas. I don't need you anymore!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: I know. I need you.  I thought I was having a great time because I was getting signed by managers and, going to big sex and coke parties, but, then I realized, I was having fun because I was doing all that...  ...with my best friend.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: You know, I'm just, I'm just through with this whole thing! I just, I can't put my family through any more of this!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: S-so you did superglue it.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Ugh, you know what's really sad?! That this is what matters to people! What nobody seems to remember are the farmers in Belarus! STANground is bigger than me! You wanna tell all those farmers in Belarus it's all bullcrap?!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Dude, the sooner you eat your peas, the sooner you can try to win some of your friends back. Eh ya you need to Bill Clinton this.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: You really think I went through all that trouble, lying about my wristband and claiming to be something I wasn't, for what, Kyle?! For what?!  This is a witch hunt, dude! It's all that stupid French scientist! He's the fraud, and I'm gonna prove it!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Good morning South Park Elementary. These are the morning announcements. And I, am Eric Cartman. All forms for the school book drive must be handed it to Mr. Davis in the library by the end of the school day today. Hm, doesn't seem like they gave us a lot of notice on that, uh, 'kay. Oh well.  For lunch today, the cafeteria will be serving a selection of cold sandwiches. Cold sandwiches. Oh well, thank you so much! Remember when we used to be served hot food? I mean, what has happened to our school? This school is transforming into something very bad, and why? Because we have leadership that doesn't care! I'm talking of course about our student body president, Wendy Testaburger!  Ever since Wendy was elected student body president, this school has started a rapid decline towards some socialist regime where students no longer have a voice! The music room will be closed off today due to painting. All students in band and choir will meet in the gymnasium instead. Oh oh, so now, so now the school is using money to paint the music room. How old and outdated is our playground equipment? What other school has a fifteen-year-old merry-go-round on it? Our school president is turning this whole place into Communist Russia!  It's not a coincidence that once Wendy took office this school started coming apart at the seams! Your teachers don't wanna tell you, but they are scared, and they should be, because the very fabric of this elementary school is tearing from all corners.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Oh jeez!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: But hey, I'm just a normal kid, like you, except that I ask questions. And because I'm brave enough to ask questions, I come under scrutinies. Is Wendy using your lunch money to buy heroin? Probably not, but how can we know? I don't want my lunch money going to drugs! Who's taking these drugs? What would be the point? I'm asking questions!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I don't now what to do. Kids are starting to make fun of me because, I'm no good at sarcastaball. I suck at being nice and polite. I'm so good at sucking I should work at a Thai massage parlor.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Uh-uh oh, no E-eric, you're, why you're a great player.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: No, I just don't have the mojo you have, Butters.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Oh, well, ye-Eric, ah I told you: everyone has a creamy filling inside them, where all the feelings of compassion and joy come from. Oh, didn't your dad ever tell you that?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: ...I don't have a dad.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: ...Oh right, I, I'm sorry. Well, everyone has a creamy filling, Eric. And and some people have so much of that filling that it, that it comes out sometimes. A lot of times, when I go to sleep, and especially if I've had them wonderful dreams that make me feel really good, sometimes I wake up, and when I wake up, I I realize some of my goo has come out.  But I always make sure I keep it, just in case I ever run out of all my sunshine happy feelings.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Wow, Eric. You sure are a great guy for doing this.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I don't have a choice. Somebody has to get into San Francisco and warn Kyle's family to get out before the storm hits.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: But... how come we can't just take the bus on into the city?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You don't know San Francisco, Butters.  It was the breeding ground for the hippie movement in the 60s. Those hard-core liberals, lesbian activists, and die hard modern hippies young and old.  I swore I would never set foot in San Francisco. God help me.  All right, Butters, I'll be tethered to you through this cord. It's my only lifeline, so make sure it stays taut. If you stop hearing my voice, for the love of Christ, start reeling me in with all your strength.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: I will. You're really great for going to warn Kyle, Eric.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I'm not doin' it for Kyle.  I can't believe I'm actually going to walk through San Francisco. Well, here it goes.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Oh, well, ye-Eric, ah I told you: everyone has a creamy filling inside them, where all the feelings of compassion and joy come from. Oh, didn't your dad ever tell you that?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: ...I don't have a dad.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: ...Oh right, I, I'm sorry. Well, everyone has a creamy filling, Eric. And and some people have so much of that filling that it, that it comes out sometimes. A lot of times, when I go to sleep, and especially if I've had them wonderful dreams that make me feel really good, sometimes I wake up, and when I wake up, I I realize some of my goo has come out.  But I always make sure I keep it, just in case I ever run out of all my sunshine happy feelings.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Youuu save it all?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: My goo doesn't come out every night, but I sure do seem to have a surplus of it.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Butters, do you think your goo might work on someone else?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: No, I just don't have the mojo you have, Butters.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Oh, well, ye-Eric, ah I told you: everyone has a creamy filling inside them, where all the feelings of compassion and joy come from. Oh, didn't your dad ever tell you that?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: ...I don't have a dad.

lines.text: ...Oh right, I, I'm sorry. Well, everyone has a creamy filling, Eric. And and some people have so much of that filling that it, that it comes out sometimes. A lot of times, when I go to sleep, and especially if I've had them wonderful dreams that make me feel really good, sometimes I wake up, and when I wake up, I I realize some of my goo has come out.  But I always make sure I keep it, just in case I ever run out of all my sunshine happy feelings.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Youuu save it all?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: My goo doesn't come out every night, but I sure do seem to have a surplus of it.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Why can't you leave it alone?! Why can't you just stop?!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Because, Butters, Kyle was spying on us, okay-  Kyle was spying on us, and he knows that we put the video of Craig's mom on the Internet. He found out everything. That's why I had to make another video.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Because when Kyle said he heard us say we made the video, we can say we were talking about THIS video. "Ohh, thank you Eric, that's pretty smart. I really owe you one."

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: That poor lady. Her vagina is everywhere. Poor Craig.

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Butters, we live in a world where privacy is gone. Okay? It's... It' just gone, bud. Your wiener, my balls, they're public domain. You can get on the Internet right now and look at that chick from Hunger Games' butthole. We can either all freak out about it, or we can just calm down because honestly, I'm getting a little sick of covering your ass!

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Really glad you joined the company, Annie! Now remember: you're chargin' five dollars for a kiss, two for a hug, and six for half and half. Alright bitch? Alright, how about a freebie?  Ohwoah,  whoopie!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, I really think we should talk.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Sure, Kyle.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, can't you see this is wrong? You've got little boys all over school spending all their lunch money on kisses. Boys shouldn't be paying for kisses. It's wrong.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Kyle, every boy pays for kisses. Do you know what I am saying? If you've got a girl, and she kisses you, sooner or later you're paying for it. You've gotta take her out to lunch, take her to a movie, and then spend time listenin' to all her stupid problems. Look, look at Stan right there.  He's gotta sit there and listen to her stupid motherfuckin' problems 'cause she kisses him. If you ask me, that's a lot more than the five dollars my company charges.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, what's happened to you?

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, I really think we should talk.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Sure, Kyle.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, can't you see this is wrong? You've got little boys all over school spending all their lunch money on kisses. Boys shouldn't be paying for kisses. It's wrong.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: What happened is that I became a man! I'm sorry I'm not your little buddy anymore, but there's a time people have to grow up!  Do you know what I am saying?!

lines.text: What happened is that I became a man! I'm sorry I'm not your little buddy anymore, but there's a time people have to grow up!  Do you know what I am saying?!

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Get out of here, all of ya!

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, people can't just go around beating up people who have diabetes! Now whatever your problem is, you just-

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: You just think you know everything, don't you Kyle?! Every little thing you gotta shoot your mouth off like you're the frickin' expert! Well you don't know everything because  your best friend is a kid who thinks the entire planet revolves around him and he only cares about HIS image!  You guys think Cartman is the only selfish piece of crap in this school? You're all fake and stuck up , and none of you have the courage to tell Jimmy that his jokes aren't funny!  The only kid here with any sense of dignity is Kenny, and the rest of you have your heads up your butts!

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Kyle, every boy pays for kisses. Do you know what I am saying? If you've got a girl, and she kisses you, sooner or later you're paying for it. You've gotta take her out to lunch, take her to a movie, and then spend time listenin' to all her stupid problems. Look, look at Stan right there.  He's gotta sit there and listen to her stupid motherfuckin' problems 'cause she kisses him. If you ask me, that's a lot more than the five dollars my company charges.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Butters, what's happened to you?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: What happened is that I became a man! I'm sorry I'm not your little buddy anymore, but there's a time people have to grow up!  Do you know what I am saying?!

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Grandma? I did it, Grandma.  I finally stood up for myself. I got real mean and I beat the shnozz our of Dr. Oz.  I can't lie, it felt kind of good.  At first. But since then all I have is just... a kind of dark, empty feeling. Then I realized... that's how you must feel. All the time.  Poor old Grandma. You know, I've I've been gettin' lots of advice how to deal with you. Stand up to you, tell on you... But I kind of realize there's just people like you out there. All over the place. When you're a kid, things seem like they're gonna last forever. But they're not. Life changes. Why you won't always be around. Someday you're gonna die.  Someday pretty soon.  And when you're layin' in that hospital bed, with tubes up your nose, and that little pan under your butt to pee in, well I'll come visit ya. I'll come just to show you that, that I'm still alive and I'm still happy. And you'll die. Bein' nothin' but you.  'Night Grandma.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Huh? Aw, come on!  Dude, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: I always thought, someday when I grow up I'm gonna get a Harley. Then people will have to notice me, and they'll have to deal with my shit for once. The open road! The wind on my face! I'll go from city to city! Rowrrowrroowwrr! Everyone lookin' at me! "Who's that guy? He must be a Hoo- he must be tough!" Vromvrom, vromvrom, vrooom! I'll have my girl on the back seat holdin' on to my fat belly - sure she's missin' a few teeth, but she thinks I'm coool. That's why, when I grow up, I'm gonna be a Harley rider!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: ...That makes perfect sense, Butters.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: It does?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Yes, now get out of here.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Alright, Kyle and I are gonna spray paint messages to the Harley riders; Cartman, you said you had an idea?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: I told you, I can't stand my future self. I have to do whatever I can to not become a loser like him.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Well, studying is the golden key to the imposing door of success.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: I just can't stand having my future self around all the time! It's driving me crazy!  Maybe if I get smarter I won't become him and I won't have to share my room!

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: I know what you mean. I hate having my future self around, too.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Yeah, it's like everything I do, he...  Wait a minute, what'd you say?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: I said, I know how you feel. My life has gone completely downhill ever since my future self moved in. I hate him! All he ever wants to do is watch Becker. And that show is so stupid.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: And eleven if you count the two family members that killed themselves afterward.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Aaaah!

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: But that isn't the point, Butters!  The point is that this is now! It's on! And there are people who need you to step up! Look, nobody likes having to rise to a challenge. But competing against other people and getting in their faces saying "Haha! I'm better than you!" is part of life. And if you can't face that, then you might as well sit here and play Legos until you're an old man.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Get out of my room, Stan.

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Fine.  But someday you're gonna have to stop running from what happened and start dealing with it. Otherwise, you might as well move to France with all the other pussies.

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Well, studying is the golden key to the imposing door of success.

lines.text: Dude, how long has your future self been around?

lines.speaker: Stan
lines.text: Dude, how long has your future self been around?

lines.speaker: Butters
lines.text: Oh. I guess it's been around four months now.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Yes. What's up, my lord?)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I'm not sure if his heart is in the right place. If he were to ever...  switch sides,  it could make Stan's army problematic for us.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (But Stan and Kyle are best friends)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: True, but we can't let Kyle come in the way of what's rightfully ours.  Let's face it, Lady McCormick, this is really about you and me getting XBox Ones. The others are simply there to... help us get inside those doors.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Yes. That's right.)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: You have a strong influence over the rest of the men, Lady McCormick. All I'm saying is when the time comes, I might need you to... use that influence to have Kyle taken care of. Do we understand each other?

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: I'm not sure if his heart is in the right place. If he were to ever...  switch sides,  it could make Stan's army problematic for us.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Yes, I believe we do.)

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Dude, what the fuck are you doing?!)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Come in dude, I wanna show you what I've been workin' on.  Check it out. I thought I could just give all my money away and be as poor as your family, but then I realized: what do poor people do? Buy things even though they don't have money by going out and purchasing things that are 0% down and no payments for two years! That's how you people stay poor forever! Am I right? That's it, right?

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Dude, fuck you!)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Kenny, I'm just trying to get good at NASCAR.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Being poor has nothing to do with NASCAR!)

lines.speaker: Cartman
lines.text: Hey, I love NASCAR just as much as you do!

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Oh that fucking asshole!)  (Oh that fucking asshole, I'm gonna talk to him...)

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Kyle, I'm gonna camp and watch the meteor shower. Do you wanna come and see it with me?)

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: I can't watch the meteor shower with you, Kenny. I have to go to Jewbilee.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (What's that?)

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: It's what we do in Jew Scouts. Usually we just sit around and make stuff. But tonight, because there's a meteor shower, we're gonna do some big thing out in the woods. It's gonna suck ass, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Oh, that's alright.)

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Hey! Maybe you can come with me. Then it won't suck so hard.

lines.text: Oh! Hey, Kenny.

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: I know! But that doesn't matter any more! These kids don't wanna play the game, they just wanna watch people on YouTube play it. And you know why? Commentary.

lines.speaker: Kenny
lines.text: (Commentary?)

lines.speaker: Kyle
lines.text: Yeah. You used to sit in the living room with family and friends to hear their commentary on things. Now you can get it with a little guy in a window on the screen. Someone who just comments on stuff. I mean, really? Who would even WANT to do that?!
